level_signals: 8–12 years of experience in Product Management
at least 3–4 years leading Trust & Safety, Fraud, or Risk product teams
Most senior T&S product leader at Scale, reporting directly to the Head of GenAI Product
Comp range $240K–$300K base consistent with Senior/Principal PM at a high-growth tech company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8–12 years of experience in Product Management in the tech industry, with at least 3–4 years leading Trust & Safety, Fraud, or Risk product teams
at least 3–4 years leading Trust & Safety, Fraud, or Risk product teams

Deep domain expertise in fraud (not security) — specifically consumer product fraud, marketplace fraud, or contributor/gig-economy fraud. FinTech/payments fraud alone is not sufficient; we need someone who understands platform-level abuse and adversarial user behavior
Track record of building T&S systems at consumer-facing marketplace companies (e.g., Uber, DoorDash, Airbnb, eBay, Instacart) or similar high-growth platforms with global contributor/user bases
Proven ability to balance fraud mitigation with growth protection — you've made hard tradeoff decisions between blocking bad actors and preserving good UX

Exceptional stakeholder management skills — you've navigated complex orgs with Ops, Engineering, and leadership stakeholders who have competing priorities and strong opinions. You handle escalations, build trust, and drive alignment independently
Strong analytical capabilities — you can investigate contributor behavior data, define fraud metrics, and build detection logic. High proficiency in SQL
Experience setting ambitious product vision and executing against it — you've moved a function from reactive to proactive
Bachelor's or advanced degree in a quantitative, engineering, or related discipline

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building T&S systems with global/international coverage across diverse contributor populations
Understanding of AI-enabled fraud vectors and how adversarial behavior evolves as AI capabilities improve
Experience with contributor/gig-economy platforms specifically (not just consumer marketplaces)
Experience working at early-to-growth-stage companies where T&S was being built or rebuilt from scratch

explicitly_excluded: FinTech/payments fraud alone is not sufficient; we need someone who understands platform-level abuse and adversarial user behavior

scope.surface: Trust & Safety product platform for Scale AI's global contributor marketplace — fraud detection, prevention, remediation, Risk Engine, data signals intelligence, anomaly detection, contributor investigation tooling
scope.business_model: two-sided marketplace (AI labs/enterprises as demand side; 500K+ contributors across 100+ countries as supply side)
core_responsibilities: Set the product vision and strategy for Trust & Safety at Scale, shifting the function from reactive incident response to proactive, intelligence-driven fraud prevention
Own the product roadmap for fraud detection, prevention, and remediation systems — including the Risk Engine, data signals intelligence, anomaly detection, and contributor investigation tooling
Build a balanced approach to fraud mitigation and growth protection
Investigate and deeply understand contributor behavior patterns to identify emerging fraud vectors before they become material
Drive cross-functional alignment across Ops, EPD, Finance, and executive leadership
Translate complex fraud patterns into clear product requirements for engineering, and partner with data science to build and improve detection models
Build long-term instrumentation, monitoring, and evaluation capabilities to measure fraud rates, false positive rates, contributor impact, and financial exposure
Establish business cases quantifying fraud losses and ROI of prevention investments
Partner with Finance and Delivery Ops leadership to manage the financial impact of fraud on business profitability

compensation.equity_form: equity grant (subject to Board of Director approval)

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($240K–$300K base) plus equity
Clear product ownership — most senior T&S PM, reporting to Head of GenAI Product
Dedicated engineering, data science, and operations partners stated explicitly
High-autonomy, high-impact role with explicit scope defined
Honest about current state of T&S (reactive/patchwork) — sets realistic expectations
Specific domain exclusions stated (FinTech-only fraud not sufficient) — signals intentional hiring
red_flags: Role requires being in SF, NYC, or Seattle — no remote option mentioned, fully eliminating remote candidates
Visa sponsorship not mentioned anywhere in the JD
T&S described as immature and fire-fighting — 'fires happen weekly' may indicate high operational burden
8–12 year experience band is narrow and combined with very specific domain (marketplace fraud T&S) makes candidate pool small
No people management explicitly stated but role is most senior T&S PM — team structure and growth path unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, years of experience required, and 'most senior T&S product leader at Scale' language)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not listed as KPIs in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description of two-sided marketplace)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from pay transparency statement listing only SF, NYC, Seattle; not explicitly stated)
ambiguities: Location type: JD body references pay transparency for SF, NYC, and Seattle but does not explicitly say 'on-site only' or 'no remote'. Treated as onsite given no remote language is present.
Visa sponsorship: not mentioned anywhere in the JD; marked as 'unclear'.
manages_people: role is described as 'most senior T&S product leader' with dedicated partners, but no direct reports mentioned — left as false but ambiguous.

Severity of education requirement: phrased as part of 'Ideally, you'd have' list which could imply soft, but quantitative/engineering degree alongside deep analytical requirements suggests hard expectation — classified as hard.
